reason: Applying 'noexec' to /home prevents any binary or script from being executed directly from that partition. This can break user-space applications, custom scripts, local development environments, or software installed in user directories (e.g., Python virtualenvs, local binaries in ~/bin). While it is a standard hardening step, it carries a high risk of breaking user workflows and requires verification of how users interact with the /home partition.
estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Prevents execution of scripts and binaries in /home; May break user-installed software or development environments; May break application-specific binaries stored in user home directories
